meta.name: PSC-17 Pediatric Symptom Checklist
meta.description: The PSC-17 is a 17-item brief screening tool for assessing internalizing, attention, and externalizing problems in children and adolescents.
meta.author: Jellinek MS, et al. (1988); Gardner W, et al. (1999)
meta.reference: Gardner W, et al. The PSC-17: A brief pediatric symptom checklist with psychosocial problem subscales. Ambulatory Child Health. 1999;5:225-236.
meta.instruction: Please mark how often each statement applies to your child in the past month.

questions.text: Is down on him or herself.
Seems to be having less fun.
Acts as if driven by a motor.
Does not understand other people's feelings.
Blames others for his or her troubles.
Takes things that do not belong to him or her.

ranges.label: Normal Range
ranges.description: Total score is within normal range. No significant psychosocial problems detected. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue monitoring your child's mental health
Maintain good parent-child communication
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities

ranges.label: Clinically Significant
ranges.description: Total score reaches clinically significant level. Further evaluation is recommended. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Consult a child mental health professional for detailed evaluation
Review subscale scores to identify specific problem areas
Communicate with teachers about the child's school behavior
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques

internalizing.label: Normal
internalizing.description: Internalizing problems within normal range. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

internalizing.label: Elevated
internalizing.description: Elevated internalizing problems, possible emotional distress. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

attention.label: Normal
attention.description: Attention problems within normal range. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

attention.label: Elevated
attention.description: Elevated attention problems, further evaluation may be needed. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

externalizing.label: Normal
externalizing.description: Externalizing problems within normal range. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

externalizing.label: Elevated
externalizing.description: Elevated externalizing problems, possible behavioral issues. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life
